description: Premade architectures — the composition handbook's worked answers, one factory call each, with wiring diagrams generated from the code that builds them.
---

<span class="nb-kicker">Build</span>

# Architectures

The factories in `resdag.models` are the composition handbook's worked
answers. Each one is a short function that wires
[layers](../layers/index.md) into a model exactly the way you would by
hand, then returns it ready to train — and what comes back is an ordinary
model, not a sealed product. Retrain it on your data, swap its topology,
embed it in a larger network, or open its source and use it as a template.
The current catalog is ESN-family; the pattern is not, and nothing about
the composition system ends where the premades do.

When no premade fits, write your own. A custom factory is nothing more
than a function that wires a graph and returns a model — six lines,
import included — and it drops into every workflow (training, forecasting,
tuning) exactly as the premades do:

<div class="nb-specimen" data-label="my_factory.py" markdown>

```python
import resdag as rd

def my_esn(n: int, dim: int) -> rd.ESNModel:
    inp = rd.reservoir_input(dim)
    states = rd.ESNLayer(n, feedback_size=dim)(inp)
    return rd.ESNModel(inp, rd.CGReadoutLayer(n, dim, name="output")(states))
```

</div>

<!-- nb-cards: build/architectures -->

Each architecture page shows the actual wiring diagram, generated by
`plot_model` from the factory itself at build time. The figure is rendered
from the same code that constructs your model, so it cannot drift from the
code — if the wiring changes, the diagram changes with it.

## See also

- [Layers](../layers/index.md) — the parts every factory is wired from.
- [Train](../../workflows/train.md) — fitting any of these with one `ESNTrainer.fit` call.
- [Models reference](../../reference/models.md) — full factory signatures.
